pattern: At every step only the first three survivors matter, and after each move at most one of them lingers into the next round -- so the whole process reduces to a DP over (how far the array has been consumed, which single leftover value is still waiting to be paired).
hints: However you interleave the removals, at any moment the untouched part of the array is always a plain suffix, plus at most one earlier element that got spared in the previous round.
That means the whole state you need to remember is just two things: where the untouched suffix begins, and which earlier value (if any) is still waiting to be paired off.

From a state with no waiting value, look at the next three entries and try discarding each of the three possible pairs (paying the larger of the two), carrying the third forward as the new waiting value; from a state with a waiting value, do the same among {waiting value, next two entries}. Once fewer than three items remain overall, the whole remainder is removed in one move at a cost of its own maximum.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a tie among the top two
two elements, removed together in one move
exactly three elements: one pair-removal plus a final single removal
already increasing, a greedy that always keeps the smallest survivor can be led astray
every value identical, so cost is forced regardless of which pairs are chosen
duplicated extremes, tests that tie-breaking never lowers the cost incorrectly

stress.description: random array across the full length and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
